---
title: "density_food"
author: "Ruwen Zhou"
date: "9/2/2024"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(readr)
library(plyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(DESeq2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggpubr)
```

```{r}
food_data = read_xlsx("data/FFQA862.xlsx")
nutrition_data = read_xlsx("data/FFQB800.xlsx")
metadata = read_xlsx("data/ethnicity_866.xlsx")

food_data[] <- lapply(food_data, function(x) as.double(as.character(x)))
food_data <- food_data %>%
  mutate(Participant_ID = as.character(Participant_ID))
food_data
nutrition_data
metadata
```

```{r}
ethnicity_colors <- c("C" = "#9FDDCA", "I" = "#9EA3F1", "M" = "#E17887", "O" = "#80796BFF")

merged_data <- food_data %>%
  inner_join(metadata[,c("FREG1_Barcode", "FREG5_Ethnic_Group")], by = c("Participant_ID" = "FREG1_Barcode"))

# Filter out the 'O' ethnic group and select relevant columns
plot_data <- merged_data %>%
  filter(FREG5_Ethnic_Group != "O") %>%
  select(FREG5_Ethnic_Group, everything(), -Participant_ID)

# Get the names of all food columns
food_columns <- names(plot_data)[names(plot_data) != "FREG5_Ethnic_Group"]

# Create a directory to store the plots
dir.create("food_density_plots", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Loop through each food group and create individual density plots
for (food in food_columns) {
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = .data[[food]], fill = FREG5_Ethnic_Group)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = ethnicity_colors) +
    labs(title = paste(food),
         x = "Consumption",
         y = "Density",
         fill = "Ethnic Group") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
          axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
          legend.title = element_text(size = 12),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 10))
  
  # Save the plot
  ggsave(paste0("figure/food_density_plots/", food, "_density_plot.png"), 
         plot = p, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
  
  # Print progress
  cat("Saved plot for", food, "\n")
}

cat("All plots have been saved in the 'food_density_plots' directory.\n")
```

```{r}
library(rstatix)
stat_results <- data.frame(Food = character(),
                           KW_statistic = numeric(),
                           KW_p_value = numeric(),
                           KW_p_adjusted = numeric(),
                           Significant_pairs = character(),
                           stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Loop through each food group
for (food in food_columns) {
  # Kruskal-Wallis test
  kw_test <- plot_data %>%
    kruskal_test(as.formula(paste(food, "~ FREG5_Ethnic_Group")))
  
  # Store results (without adjusted p-value for now)
  stat_results <- stat_results %>%
    add_row(Food = food,
            KW_statistic = kw_test$statistic,
            KW_p_value = kw_test$p,
            KW_p_adjusted = NA,
            Significant_pairs = "")
  
  # Print progress
  cat("Processed", food, "\n")
}

# Adjust p-values using Benjamini-Hochberg method
stat_results$KW_p_adjusted <- p.adjust(stat_results$KW_p_value, method = "BH")

# Perform Dunn's test for foods with significant adjusted p-values
for (i in 1:nrow(stat_results)) {
  if (stat_results$KW_p_adjusted[i] < 0.05) {
    food <- stat_results$Food[i]
    dunn_test <- plot_data %>%
      dunn_test(as.formula(paste(food, "~ FREG5_Ethnic_Group")), p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")
    
    # Get significant pairs
    sig_pairs <- dunn_test %>%
      filter(p.adj < 0.05) %>%
      mutate(pair = paste(group1, group2, sep="-")) %>%
      pull(pair) %>%
      paste(collapse = ", ")
    
    stat_results$Significant_pairs[i] <- ifelse(sig_pairs == "", "None", sig_pairs)
  } else {
    stat_results$Significant_pairs[i] <- "None"
  }
}

# Save statistical results
write.csv(stat_results, "figure/food_statistical_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("Statistical results have been saved in 'food_statistical_results.csv'.\n")

# Print summary of significant differences
cat("\nSummary of significant differences (after FDR correction):\n")
stat_results %>%
  filter(KW_p_adjusted < 0.05) %>%
  select(Food, KW_p_adjusted, Significant_pairs) %>%
  arrange(KW_p_adjusted) %>%
  print(n = Inf)

# Print foods with no significant differences
cat("\nFoods with no significant differences among ethnic groups (after FDR correction):\n")
stat_results %>%
  filter(KW_p_adjusted >= 0.05) %>%
  pull(Food) %>%
  cat(sep = ", ")
```
